content:
BillWise is an open source bill management application for restaurants with meal and drink entries, service charges, discounts, and sales reports, built-in Python and Flask.
Sales reports feature data analytics and data visualisations to quickly understand sales trends and operational insights for informed decision-making.
To do
Fix insert each paid bill into the CSV file
Fix display visualisations of the sales report
Deploy the app on Heroku
Adjust the size of the buttons to ensure they display correctly on screens of varying sizes.
summary:
BillWise is an open source bill management application for restaurants with meal and drink entries, service charges, discounts, and sales reports, built-in Python and Flask.
Sales reports feature data analytics and data visualisations to quickly understand sales trends and operational insights for informed decision-making.
To do
Fix insert each paid bill into the CSV file
Fix display visualisations of the sales report
Deploy the app on Heroku
Adjust the size of the buttons to ensure they display correctly on screens of varying sizes.
title: Billwise

content: This project reimagines “Exquisite Corpse” for the digital age, allowing two writers to co-create a story in turns, with each seeing only the other’s last sentence. It’s a novel and dynamic approach to collaborative storytelling.
summary: This project reimagines “Exquisite Corpse” for the digital age, allowing two writers to co-create a story in turns, with each seeing only the other’s last sentence. It’s a novel and dynamic approach to collaborative storytelling.
title: Corpse

content: One goal of Creative Commons is to increase the amount of openly licensed creativity in “the commons” — the body of work freely available for legal use, sharing, repurposing, and remixing. Through the use of CC licenses, millions of people around the world have made their photos, videos, writing, music, and other creative content available for any member of the public to use.
summary: One goal of Creative Commons is to increase the amount of openly licensed creativity in “the commons” — the body of work freely available for legal use, sharing, repurposing, and remixing.
